name: Dhammacakkappavattana Sutta
shortDescription: The Dhammacakkappavattana Sutta, often translated as 'Setting in Motion the Wheel of Dharma Sutta', is the Buddha's first sermon after his enlightenment.
description: The Dhammacakkappavattana Sutta is a foundational text in Buddhism, marking the formal beginning of the Buddha's teaching ministry. Delivered to his former ascetic companions at Sarnath, near Varanasi, it outlines the core principles of the Four Noble Truths and the Noble Eightfold Path. The sutta rejects both the extreme of sensual indulgence and the extreme of self-mortification, advocating instead for the Middle Way, which leads to peace, insight, enlightenment, and Nirvana. The Four Noble Truths, as presented in the sutta, address the nature of suffering (dukkha), its origin (samudaya), its cessation (nirodha), and the path to its cessation (magga). The Noble Eightfold Path, the practical guide to ending suffering, encompasses right understanding, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right concentration. The sutta emphasizes the importance of personal experience and verification of these truths, rather than blind faith. The Dhammacakkappavattana Sutta is considered crucial for understanding the fundamental doctrines of Buddhism and serves as a blueprint for the spiritual journey towards liberation.

sources.title: The Middle Length Discourses of the Buddha: A Translation of the Majjhima Nikāya
sources.author: Bhikkhu Bodhi
